task_completion.description: Verify that a new folder named 'Build-Alerts' was created in the email client.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the folder pane (usually the left sidebar) of the email client. A folder exactly named 'Build-Alerts' should be visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that emails containing the specified keywords in their subjects were moved to the 'Build-Alerts' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look inside the 'Build-Alerts' folder. The email list should display messages with subjects containing 'fail', 'error', 'bug', or 'problem' (case-insensitive).

task_completion.description: Verify that the emails in the 'Build-Alerts' folder are marked as Unread.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the emails listed in the 'Build-Alerts' folder. They should have visual indicators of being unread, such as bold text, a closed envelope icon, or an unread dot.

task_completion.description: Verify that the most recent email in the 'Build-Alerts' folder was forwarded to the correct recipient.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an email compose window or a message in the 'Sent' folder addressed to 'build-master@company.com'. The quoted original message should be the one with the latest timestamp from the 'Build-Alerts' folder.

task_completion.description: Verify that the forwarded email contains the exact required message.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the body of the forwarded email in the compose window or sent view. It must contain the exact text: 'Critical build failure detected. Please investigate immediately.'

task_completion.description: Verify that the exact Subject lines of all moved emails were logged to ~/Documents/triage_log.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or a terminal output showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/triage_log.txt'. The file should contain a list of email subject lines, one per line, corresponding to the emails moved to the 'Build-Alerts' folder.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used a graphical email client to manage the emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show an actual email application (like Thunderbird or Evolution) being used to create folders, move emails, and forward messages, rather than terminal scripts bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: Ensure the forwarding action was performed via the email client interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the email client's compose window or sent confirmation, proving the agent didn't just use a background command-line tool (like curl or mail) to send the email.

integrity.description: Ensure the log file contains actual subject lines extracted from the emails.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in triage_log.txt should match the actual subjects of the filtered emails seen in the email client, not generic placeholders or copy-pasted instructions from the task description.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target folder: 'Build-Alerts'. Keywords to filter: 'fail', 'error', 'bug', 'problem'. Escalation recipient: 'build-master@company.com'. Log file path: '/home/ga/Documents/triage_log.txt'.
